prompt:
I teach two sections of CS101 and my grade spreadsheet has become self-aware. Please build me a gradebook CLI. Python stdlib only. The grade MATH must live in a pure module `grading.py` (I will unit-test it against our registrar's numbers), CLI entry point `cli.py`, persistence in a JSON file passed as `--db PATH` to every command; add a store/report module split as you see fit.

Setup commands:
- `add-category NAME --weight W [--drop-lowest N]` -- W is a positive number (they do NOT have to sum to 100, see renormalization below), N defaults to 0. Duplicate category: nonzero exit + stderr.
- `add-assignment NAME --category CAT --points P` -- P a positive number. Unknown category or duplicate assignment name: error.
- `record STUDENT ASSIGNMENT SCORE` -- overwrites on re-record. Score must be >= 0 (extra credit past max is allowed). Unknown assignment: error. A 'known student' is anyone with at least one recorded score.
- `curve LETTER=MIN [LETTER=MIN ...]` -- replaces the whole curve, e.g. `curve A=90 B+=87 B=80 C=70 D=60`. Malformed specs: error. Until a curve is set the default is A=90 B=80 C=70 D=60. Anything below every threshold is an F.

Grading rules (the part I actually care about, all in grading.py):
- `category_percent(pairs, drop_lowest)` -- pairs is [(earned, possible)]. Drop the `drop_lowest` entries with the LOWEST earned/possible ratio (never drop everything: at most len-1 get dropped), then the category score is sum(earned)/sum(possible)*100 over what survives. Points-weighted, not an average of percentages.
- Missing work counts as an explicit (0, possible) pair -- a student who skipped hw2 has it in their pairs as a zero, and yes it can then be dropped by the policy.
- `weighted_final(items)` -- items is [(percent, weight)]; result is the weight-RENORMALIZED average (so weights 40/60/10 divide by 110). Categories that have no assignments yet contribute nothing and are excluded before renormalizing.
- `letter(percent, curve)` -- curve is {letter: min_percent}; the letter with the highest threshold <= percent, else 'F'. The letter is judged on the DISPLAY value, i.e. the final rounded to one decimal.

Reports:
- `student NAME` -- one line per non-empty category sorted by category name, then the total: `CAT<TAB>P%` then `total<TAB>P%<TAB>LETTER`, all percents one decimal (e.g. `homework<TAB>95.0%`). Unknown student: error.
- `assignment NAME` -- `NAME<TAB>K scored<TAB>avg A%<TAB>min M%<TAB>max X%` over recorded scores as percents of possible, one decimal; plus a second line `missing<TAB>s1,s2` (known students without a score, sorted) only when somebody is missing. Unknown assignment: error.

Separate process per command, state through the db file. Acceptance: `python3 test_gradebook.py`, which you must not edit.
